title: heptapleurum rostratum
intro: Heptapleurum rostratum, also known as the fingered fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Heptapleurum rostratum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and treat digestive issues. Heptapleurum rostratum is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Southeast Asian medicine.
